plan.description:
Pēdējo divdesmit gadu laikā digitālās platformas - IT uzņēmumi, kas veido un uztur digitālas infrastruktūras cilvēku saskarsmei un dažādu pakalpojumu sniegšanai - ir kļuvušas par ekonomikas un sadzīves neatņemamu sastāvdaļu visā pasaulē. Šo uzņēmumu ietekme ir ne tikai ekonomiska, bet arī sociāli strukturāla. Pētījuma “Digitālajās platformās nodarbināto autonomijas izpratnes un prakse: Wolt un Bolt piegādes darbinieku pieredzes kultūrsocioloģiska analīze”, kas veikts no 2022.gada 1.janvārim līdz 2023.gada 31.decembrim, mērķis bija gūt padziļinātu izpratni par vienu šīs jaunās platformu ekonomikas aspektu – platformu darbu (platform labour arī gig work), padziļināti analizējot vienu no visplašāk izplatītākajiem un redzamākajiem platforma darba veidiem – ēdienu piegādes darbu, un kā empīrisko piemēru skatot Rīgā strādājošos ēdienu piegādes kurjerus platformās Wolt un Bolt Food. Pētījuma centrālais analītiskais jautājums bija ar šī jaunā darba formātu saistītā prekaritāte un problemātiskā attiecība starp strādājošo izjusto autonomiju un kontroli. Pētījumā tika izmantotas kvalitatīvās socioloģiskās metodes: 1) tika veiktas 60 padziļinātās intervijas ar platformās strādājošajiem; 2) tika analizēti tiešaistē pieejamie kurjeru savstarpējas saziņas forumu arhīvi (2020-2023); un 3) Bolt Food kurjeriem sūtīto ziņu arhīvs (2020-2023). Pētījuma rezultāti atspoguļoti vairākās zinātniskās publikācijās (tostarp https://doi.org/10.17645/si.7696 un https://journal.lu.lv/adz/article/view/2428).
 
 
Over the last twenty years, digital platforms—IT companies that create and maintain digital infrastructures for human interaction and the provision of various services—have become an integral part of the global economy and daily life. The influence of these companies is not only economic but also socially structural. The aim of the research project “The Meaning and Practice of Autonomy in Gig Work: Sociocultural Inquiry in Experience of Wolt and Bolt Delivery Workers in Riga” (conducted from January 1, 2022, to December 31, 2023) was to gain a deeper understanding of one aspect of this new platform economy – gig work – through empirical analysis of food delivery couriers working in Riga for the Wolt and Bolt Food platforms. The central analytical question of the study was the precarity associated with this new form of work and the problematic relationship between the perceived autonomy of the workers and the control they are subject to. The research utilized qualitative sociological methods: 1) 60 in-depth interviews with platform workers were conducted; and 2) analysis of communication among couriers in Telegram forums; 3) analysis of message sent by Bolt Food to its couriers. The results of the study have been reflected in several scientific publications (including https://doi.org/10.17645/si.7696 and https://journal.lu.lv/adz/article/view/2428).
plan.title: FLPP GigWork DPP

systemField.label: Description
systemField.description: Briefly describe the context and purpose of the DMP

systemField.label: Language
systemField.description: Select the language of your DMP

referenceField.label: Researchers
referenceField.description: Add here the names of people that have produced, processed, analysed the data described in the DMP.

referenceField.label: Organizations
referenceField.description: Add here the names of the organizations contributing to the creation and revision of the DMPs

referenceField.label: Funding organizations
referenceField.description: Select a funder of your research or add new

referenceField.label: Grants
referenceField.description: Find the grant of your research or add new

referenceField.label: Project
referenceField.description: Projects in Argos are perceived as distinct activities falling under a grant or common activities under different grants in collaborative schemas, eg open call for contributions. Please complete it for the grant associated to your organization if your project falls under this category. In all other cases, please leave blank and it will be autocompleted.

systemField.label: Access Rights
systemField.description: Choose how the DMP is displayed after is published on Zenodo. By choosing Open Access, the DMP will be open on Zenodo after the Publication Date. By choosing Restricted Access, the DMP will be restricted after the publication is made.

extraField.label: Publication Date
extraField.description: If Open Access is selected, please indicate the date that the DMP will be made available on Zenodo.

referenceField.label: License
referenceField.description: Assign a license to your DMP by selecting the most appropriate from the list.

reference.label: Meaning and Practice of Autonomy in Gig-Work: Sociocultural Inquiry in Experience of Wolt and Bolt Delivery Workers in Riga

description.description: Datu kopā ietilpst 60 transkripti intervijām ar ēdienu piegādes platformās Wolt un Bolt Food nodarbinātajiem kurjeriem Rīgā.
description.label: Interviju ar Wolt un Bolt Food Rīgā nodarbinātajiem ēdienu kurjeriem transkripti

descriptionTemplate.label: LCS FARP
descriptionTemplate.description: This is the dataset template for FARP and SRP projects of Latvian Council of Science

fieldSet.title: What is the purpose of the data collection/generation?
fieldSet.description: Give short description of the purpose of the data collection/generation and its relation to the objectives of the project. Also give short summary of the origin of the data

selectData.label: Select the data type from the list

labelData.label: If you chose option "Others", please specify type here

fieldSet.title: Type of data generated/collected
fieldSet.description:
Type of data depends on purpose and collection/generation methods.
Dataset may consist of both primary and secondary data in some cases, however, secondary data is more re-usable as it has undergone processing and cleaning.

selectData.label: Select data format from the list

labelData.label: If you chose option "Others", please specify format here

fieldSet.title: Format of data generated/collected
fieldSet.description:
The choice of a preferred file format is crucial in order to ensure that your data will be readable also in the future. Some file formats are more likely to allow long-term readability than others are. Such formats are usually:
 - non-proprietary
 - open, with documented international standards
 - using standard character encoding, preferably Unicode (e.g. UTF-8)
 - uncompressed (space permitting)

labelData.label: Numerical value

fieldSet.title: Expected size of the data – give expected size and choose unit of measurement
Are you re-using this data set?
fieldSet.description:
Before collection of new data, researchers shall consider possibilities to re-use already prepared data within or outside their institution, thus saving human and financial resources.
If you are planning to use other researchers’ data in your research there are a couple of things that you need to take into consideration, such as copyright of datasets, permissions provided through licenses, ethical aspects for reuse, etc.

fieldSet.title: If No, please describe, if you have considered re-using any of existing data?

referenceTypeData.label: Indicate repository where the data set has been deposited

labelData.label: If repository isn't in the list, please indicate its title
If repository isn't in the list, please indicate its URL
Add persistent identifier of the data set

fieldSet.title: If Yes, please indicate what data set are you re-using?
fieldSet.description: In this case you have to fill rest of the DMP according to characteristics of the data you are re-using, as well as your plans on documenting and processing the data. If the data is stored in repositories such as Zenodo, you can auto-fill description of this dataset.

fieldSet.title: To whom the data might be useful ("data utility")? Other relevant remarks)

selectData.label: Please select the used Data Identifier System(s)

labelData.label: If you chose other, please specify the respective standard identification mechanism here

fieldSet.title: Will data be attributed with standard identification mechanism (e.g. persistent and unique identifiers such as Digital Object Identifiers – DOI)?

labelData.label: If you chose option "No", please explain why you don't plan to use any metadata

fieldSet.title: Do you plan to provide metadata for your data?
fieldSet.description: Metadata is a crucial part of data publishing, ensuring discoverability of your dataset

referenceTypeData.label: Select metadata standard from the list

labelData.label: If it's not on the list, please specify the title here
If it's not on the list, please specify URL here
If there are no standards in your discipline, describe what type of metadata will be created and how.

uploadData.label: If desired, upload an image of the used schema

fieldSet.title: Will you use any metadata standard?

labelData.label: If you will use any convention or controlled vocabulary, specify title here
If you will use any convention or controlled vocabulary, specify URL here

fieldSet.title: Will search keywords be provided that optimize possibilities for re-use?

labelData.label: If you chose option "Yes", please specify title of the naming convention
If you chose option "Yes", please specify URL

fieldSet.title: Will you follow any naming conventions for keywords?
Will you provide clear version numbers?
Other relevant remarks

options.label: open (anyone is able to access data without restrictions)
options.value: open (anyone is able to access data without restrictions)

options.label: restricted, request access (access is restricted, but request with collaboration proposal could be submitted to the authors)
options.value: restricted, request access (access is restricted, but request with collaboration proposal could be submitted to the authors)

options.label: restricted, no access (access is denied, files will be opened only through contact person to the authors)
options.value: restricted, no access (access is denied, files will be opened only through contact person to the authors)

labelData.label: If there are restrictions on use, how will access be provided? If certain datasets cannot be shared or need to be shared under restrictions, explain why. Clearly separate legal and contractual reasons from voluntary restrictions. Note that in multi-beneficiary projects it is also possible for specific beneficiaries to keep their data closed if relevant provisions are made in the consortium agreement and are in line with the reasons for opting out

fieldSet.title: What type of access the data generated/produced will have (choose one)?
Will you apply embargo period to access for your data?

referenceTypeData.label: Please choose repository from the list

labelData.label: If your repository is not on the list, please specify it's title here
If your repository is not on the list, please specify it's URL here
Please justify your choice! Have you explored appropriate arrangements with the identified repository?

fieldSet.title: Will data, associated metadata, documentation and code be made accessible with means of a repository?

labelData.label: If there is a specific software tool needed, specify it's title here
If there is a specific software tool needed, specify it's version here
If there is a specific software tool needed, specify source's URL here

fieldSet.title: What methods or software tools are needed to access the data?
Will documentation about the software needed to access the data included?
Is it possible to include the relevant software (e.g. in open source code)?
Other relevant remarks

labelData.label: Please describe what are the expected formats and to what widespread or open source code software will they be open

fieldSet.title: Are the formats open to software applications and to recombination with different data sets?

labelData.label: If yes, please specify title here
If yes, please specify URL here

fieldSet.title: Will you use data standard vocabulary/taxonomy to make your data interoperable?
Other relevant remarks

referenceTypeData.label: Please indicate the type of licence you are planning to use

labelData.label: Shortly explain your choice

fieldSet.title: How will the data be licensed to permit the widest re-use possible? Indicate the license planned to use

labelData.label: If the re-use of some or all data is restricted, explain why

fieldSet.title: Are the data usable by third parties, in particular after the end of the project?

labelData.label: If possible, indicate approximate date for that

fieldSet.title: When will the data be made available for re-use?

labelData.label: Select date until when data will be available
Please explain

fieldSet.title: How long is it intended that the data remains re-usable?
Are data quality assurance processes provided?
Other relevant remarks
How will costs be covered for making data FAIR during project realization?

referenceTypeData.label: Select from the list

labelData.label: Shortly describe distribution of tasks related to data managament process

fieldSet.title: Who will be responsible for data management in your project?
How will costs be covered for making data FAIR after project realization?

labelData.label: If you chose option "Other", please specify here
Describe these data security measures - how are you going to apply them

fieldSet.title: What security measures will be used for data security?

labelData.label: If yes, please describe them here

fieldSet.title: Are there any ethical or legal issues that could have an impact on data collection and sharing?
Have you got/will you get permission from ethics committee to collect and process data (if applicable)?
Is informed consent for data sharing and long term preservation included in questionnaires dealing with personal data, if applicable?
Will data collected/generated include personal data/ sensitive information?

options.label: Data accompanied by informed consent statements
options.value: data accompanied by informed consent statements

labelData.label: If you chose option "Other", please specify here
If data set will include personal data/sensitive information, describe methods for processing and accessing data (including anonymization/pseudonymisation of data)

fieldSet.title: If yes, what are the methods used to process and access personal data/sensitive information

field.textValue: Interviju transkripcijas to sensitīvā satura dēļ nav publiski pieejamas.

textListValues.textListValue: restricted, no access (access is denied, files will be opened only through contact person to the authors)

field.textValue: Šāda satura dati iepriekš Latvijā nav iegūti
Teksta dokuments .doc formātā, atvērt ar MS Word vai līdzīgām programmām
Starptautiski atzīta licence, piemērojama valsts pētījumu programmu ietvaros radītajiem datiem
Tiešais atbildīgais par datu ieguvi un sagatavošanu ir projekta vadītājs, par deponēšanu - datu pārvaldības speciālists
Dati glabājas Latvijas Kultūras akadēmijas mākoņserverī un ir pieejami tikai projekta pētniekiem.
Datu kopā ietilpst 60 transkripti intervijām ar ēdienu piegādes platformās Wolt un Bolt Food nodarbinātajiem kurjeriem Rīgā.

textListValues.textListValue: data accompanied by informed consent statements

field.textValue: Transkripcijās dzēsta visa informāciju, kas ļautu identificēt respondentu: respondenta vārdu un konkrētas atsauces uz vietām vai institūcijām biogrāfijā, kas ļautu respondentu identificēt.
.doc
Sociālo zinātņu pētniekiem, antropologiem, nodarbinātības pētniekiem, ekonomistiem
Dati netiks publicēti.
Personīgas, sensitīvas informācijas paušana
Dati nav publiski pieejami.

description.description: Datu kopā ietilpst 2609 ziņas, kuras laika posmā no 2023.gada 26. septembra līdz 2023.gada 31.oktobrim Telegram platformā nosūtījuši 143 grupas lietotāji. Tostarp 1940 teksta ziņas, 512 transkribētas audio ziņas, 103 attēli, 32 video, 22 digitālās uzlīmes.
description.label: Telegram kanāla ziņu arhīvs no 2023. gada 26.septembra līdz 2023. gada 31.oktobrim

field.textValue: https://zenodo.org/
Zenodo
Zenodo repozitoriju pārvalda CERN, nodrošinot mūža pieeju un ilgstamību, kā arī datu drošību

textListValues.textListValue: open (anyone is able to access data without restrictions)

field.textValue: Segtas no projekta tiešajām izmaksām pēc nepieciešamības
XLS, CSV faili, kas atverami ar programmām, kurās iespējams strādāt ar tabulāriem datiem
Starptautiski atzīta licence, piemērojama sociālo zinātņu pētījumu ietvaros radītajiem datiem
Tiešais atbildīgais par datu ieguvi un sagatavošanu ir projekta vadītājs, par deponēšanu - datu pārvaldības speciālists
Dati ir publiski pieejami
Dati ir publiski pieejami

Datu kopā ietilpst 2609 ziņas, kuras laika posmā no 2021.gada 5.jūlija līdz 2023.gada 31.oktobrim Telegram kanālā "Bolt Food Wolt Riga" nosūtījuši 143 grupas lietotāji. Tostarp 1940 teksta ziņas, 512 transkribētas audio ziņas, 103 attēli, 32 video, 22 digitālās uzlīmes.
Dati anonimizēti, lietotāju segvārdus aizvietojot identifikācijas numuriem.
Sociologiem, komunikācijas zinātniekiem
MS Excel vai citas tabulāru datu lasīšanas programmas
Segtas no projekta tiešajām izmaksām pēc nepieciešamības

description.description: Datu kopā ietilps 3486 ziņas, kuras laika posmā no 2023.gada 26.septembra līdz 2023.gada 1.novembrim Telegram kanālā nosūtījuši 59 kanāla lietotāji. Tostarp 1828 teksta ziņas, 1365 transkribētas audioziņas, 246 attēli, 36 video un 11 digitālas uzlīmes.
description.label: Telegram kanāla ziņu arhīvs no 2021. gada 5.jūlija līdz 2023. gada 31.oktobrim.

options.label: restricted, request access (access is restricted, but request with collaboration proposal could be submitted to the authors)
options.value: restricted, request access (access is restricted, but request with collaboration proposal could be submitted to the authors)

options.label: restricted, no access (access is denied, files will be opened only through contact person to the authors)
options.value: restricted, no access (access is denied, files will be opened only through contact person to the authors)

labelData.label: If there are restrictions on use, how will access be provided? If certain datasets cannot be shared or need to be shared under restrictions, explain why. Clearly separate legal and contractual reasons from voluntary restrictions. Note that in multi-beneficiary projects it is also possible for specific beneficiaries to keep their data closed if relevant provisions are made in the consortium agreement and are in line with the reasons for opting out

fieldSet.title: What type of access the data generated/produced will have (choose one)?
Will you apply embargo period to access for your data?

referenceTypeData.label: Please choose repository from the list

labelData.label: If your repository is not on the list, please specify it's title here
If your repository is not on the list, please specify it's URL here
Please justify your choice! Have you explored appropriate arrangements with the identified repository?

fieldSet.title: Will data, associated metadata, documentation and code be made accessible with means of a repository?

labelData.label: If there is a specific software tool needed, specify it's title here
If there is a specific software tool needed, specify it's version here
If there is a specific software tool needed, specify source's URL here

fieldSet.title: What methods or software tools are needed to access the data?
Will documentation about the software needed to access the data included?
Is it possible to include the relevant software (e.g. in open source code)?
Other relevant remarks

labelData.label: Please describe what are the expected formats and to what widespread or open source code software will they be open

fieldSet.title: Are the formats open to software applications and to recombination with different data sets?

labelData.label: If yes, please specify title here
If yes, please specify URL here

fieldSet.title: Will you use data standard vocabulary/taxonomy to make your data interoperable?
Other relevant remarks

referenceTypeData.label: Please indicate the type of licence you are planning to use

labelData.label: Shortly explain your choice

fieldSet.title: How will the data be licensed to permit the widest re-use possible? Indicate the license planned to use

labelData.label: If the re-use of some or all data is restricted, explain why

fieldSet.title: Are the data usable by third parties, in particular after the end of the project?

labelData.label: If possible, indicate approximate date for that

fieldSet.title: When will the data be made available for re-use?

labelData.label: Select date until when data will be available
Please explain

fieldSet.title: How long is it intended that the data remains re-usable?
Are data quality assurance processes provided?
Other relevant remarks
How will costs be covered for making data FAIR during project realization?

referenceTypeData.label: Select from the list

labelData.label: Shortly describe distribution of tasks related to data managament process

fieldSet.title: Who will be responsible for data management in your project?
How will costs be covered for making data FAIR after project realization?

labelData.label: If you chose option "Other", please specify here
Describe these data security measures - how are you going to apply them

fieldSet.title: What security measures will be used for data security?

labelData.label: If yes, please describe them here

fieldSet.title: Are there any ethical or legal issues that could have an impact on data collection and sharing?
Have you got/will you get permission from ethics committee to collect and process data (if applicable)?
Is informed consent for data sharing and long term preservation included in questionnaires dealing with personal data, if applicable?
Will data collected/generated include personal data/ sensitive information?

options.label: Data accompanied by informed consent statements
options.value: data accompanied by informed consent statements

labelData.label: If you chose option "Other", please specify here
If data set will include personal data/sensitive information, describe methods for processing and accessing data (including anonymization/pseudonymisation of data)

fieldSet.title: If yes, what are the methods used to process and access personal data/sensitive information

field.textValue: https://zenodo.org/
Zenodo
Zenodo repozitoriju pārvalda CERN, nodrošinot mūža pieeju un ilgstamību, kā arī datu drošību

textListValues.textListValue: open (anyone is able to access data without restrictions)

field.textValue: Segtas no projekta tiešajām izmaksām pēc nepieciešamības
XLS, CSV faili, kas atverami ar programmām, kurās iespējams strādāt ar tabulāriem datiem
Starptautiski atzīta licence, piemērojama sociālo zinātņu pētījumu ietvaros radītajiem datiem
Tiešais atbildīgais par datu ieguvi un sagatavošanu ir projekta vadītājs, par deponēšanu - datu pārvaldības speciālists
Dati ir publiski pieejami
Dati ir publiski pieejami

Datu kopā ietilps 3486 ziņas, kuras laika posmā no 2023.gada 26.septembra līdz 2023.gada 1.novembrim Telegram kanālā nosūtījuši 59 kanāla lietotāji. Tostarp 1828 teksta ziņas, 1365 transkribētas audioziņas, 246 attēli, 36 video un 11 digitālas uzlīmes.
Dati anonimizēti, lietotāju segvārdus aizvietojot identifikācijas numuriem.
Sociologiem, komunikācijas zinātniekiem
MS Excel vai citas tabulāru datu lasīšanas programmas
Segtas no projekta tiešajām izmaksām pēc nepieciešamības

description.description: Datu kopā ietilpst 4513 teksta ziņas, ko platforma Bolt Food nosūtījusi saviem kurjeriem laika posmā no 2020.gada 1.janvāra līdz 2023.gada 31.decembrim saziņas platformā Telegram. Uz lejuplādes brīdi kanālam bija 7230 lietotāju. Visu ziņu sūtītājs ir Bolt Food Rīga. Ziņas rakstītas latviešu un angļu valodā.
description.label: Telegram kanāla ziņu arhīvs no 2020. gada jūnija līdz 2023. gada oktobrim.

descriptionTemplate.label: LCS FARP
descriptionTemplate.description: This is the dataset template for FARP and SRP projects of Latvian Council of Science

fieldSet.title: What is the purpose of the data collection/generation?
fieldSet.description: Give short description of the purpose of the data collection/generation and its relation to the objectives of the project. Also give short summary of the origin of the data

selectData.label: Select the data type from the list

labelData.label: If you chose option "Others", please specify type here

fieldSet.title: Type of data generated/collected
fieldSet.description:
Type of data depends on purpose and collection/generation methods.
Dataset may consist of both primary and secondary data in some cases, however, secondary data is more re-usable as it has undergone processing and cleaning.

selectData.label: Select data format from the list

labelData.label: If you chose option "Others", please specify format here

fieldSet.title: Format of data generated/collected
fieldSet.description:
The choice of a preferred file format is crucial in order to ensure that your data will be readable also in the future. Some file formats are more likely to allow long-term readability than others are. Such formats are usually:
 - non-proprietary
 - open, with documented international standards
 - using standard character encoding, preferably Unicode (e.g. UTF-8)
 - uncompressed (space permitting)

labelData.label: Numerical value

fieldSet.title: Expected size of the data – give expected size and choose unit of measurement
Are you re-using this data set?
fieldSet.description:
Before collection of new data, researchers shall consider possibilities to re-use already prepared data within or outside their institution, thus saving human and financial resources.
If you are planning to use other researchers’ data in your research there are a couple of things that you need to take into consideration, such as copyright of datasets, permissions provided through licenses, ethical aspects for reuse, etc.

fieldSet.title: If No, please describe, if you have considered re-using any of existing data?

referenceTypeData.label: Indicate repository where the data set has been deposited

labelData.label: If repository isn't in the list, please indicate its title
If repository isn't in the list, please indicate its URL
Add persistent identifier of the data set

fieldSet.title: If Yes, please indicate what data set are you re-using?
fieldSet.description: In this case you have to fill rest of the DMP according to characteristics of the data you are re-using, as well as your plans on documenting and processing the data. If the data is stored in repositories such as Zenodo, you can auto-fill description of this dataset.

fieldSet.title: To whom the data might be useful ("data utility")? Other relevant remarks)

selectData.label: Please select the used Data Identifier System(s)

labelData.label: If you chose other, please specify the respective standard identification mechanism here

fieldSet.title: Will data be attributed with standard identification mechanism (e.g. persistent and unique identifiers such as Digital Object Identifiers – DOI)?

labelData.label: If you chose option "No", please explain why you don't plan to use any metadata

fieldSet.title: Do you plan to provide metadata for your data?
fieldSet.description: Metadata is a crucial part of data publishing, ensuring discoverability of your dataset

referenceTypeData.label: Select metadata standard from the list

labelData.label: If it's not on the list, please specify the title here
If it's not on the list, please specify URL here
If there are no standards in your discipline, describe what type of metadata will be created and how.

uploadData.label: If desired, upload an image of the used schema

fieldSet.title: Will you use any metadata standard?

labelData.label: If you will use any convention or controlled vocabulary, specify title here
If you will use any convention or controlled vocabulary, specify URL here

fieldSet.title: Will search keywords be provided that optimize possibilities for re-use?

labelData.label: If you chose option "Yes", please specify title of the naming convention
If you chose option "Yes", please specify URL

fieldSet.title: Will you follow any naming conventions for keywords?
Will you provide clear version numbers?
Other relevant remarks

options.label: open (anyone is able to access data without restrictions)
options.value: open (anyone is able to access data without restrictions)

options.label: restricted, request access (access is restricted, but request with collaboration proposal could be submitted to the authors)
options.value: restricted, request access (access is restricted, but request with collaboration proposal could be submitted to the authors)

options.label: restricted, no access (access is denied, files will be opened only through contact person to the authors)
options.value: restricted, no access (access is denied, files will be opened only through contact person to the authors)

labelData.label: If there are restrictions on use, how will access be provided? If certain datasets cannot be shared or need to be shared under restrictions, explain why. Clearly separate legal and contractual reasons from voluntary restrictions. Note that in multi-beneficiary projects it is also possible for specific beneficiaries to keep their data closed if relevant provisions are made in the consortium agreement and are in line with the reasons for opting out

Will you apply embargo period to access for your data?

Datu kopā ietilpst 4513 teksta ziņas, ko platforma Bolt Food nosūtījusi saviem kurjeriem laika posmā no 2020.gada 1.janvāra līdz 2023.gada 31.decembrim saziņas platformā Telegram. Uz lejuplādes brīdi kanālam bija 7230 lietotāju. Visu ziņu sūtītājs ir Bolt Food Rīga. Ziņas rakstītas latviešu un angļu valodā.
Dati anonimizēti, lietotāju segvārdus aizvietojot ar identifikācijas numuriem.
Sociologiem, komunikācijas zinātniekiem
MS Excel vai citas tabulāru datu lasīšanas programmas
Segtas no projekta tiešajām izmaksām pēc nepieciešamības
